title: Old Newtype issues which ones contain relevant Eva info
display_title: Old Newtype issues which ones contain relevant Eva info
wikitext:
[]: Old Newtype issues; which ones contain relevant Eva info?             Quote Post by V » Sat Mar 08, 2008 5:51 pm I've recently discovered that several large filing cabinets in my local anime club actually contain a vast collection of old issues of Newtype magazine. Does anyone know what might be issues that contain important Evangelion info? Or even trivial-though-interesting Eva info? I think they have both the English ones as well as the original Japanese ones.

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Sat Mar 08, 2008 6:59 pm Important/interesting Evangelion stuff should be found on important dates; either prior or upcoming Evangelion news. For example, since Rebuild was released in September, it would evidently be shown in the September issues; the earliest ones. Just remember the dates, and you'll be fine.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Mar 08, 2008 7:11 pm Yes, I would assume the '97 vintage copies to be worth a look....

[]:             Quote Post by zimlord » Sun Mar 09, 2008 12:02 am newtype's been all over eva recently. but if you're talking old issues, yeah, i'm thinking any '97 issue could deliver.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 09, 2008 12:54 pm ISSUES OF NEWTYPE?!? Eva-related stories have appeared on a regular basis in that mag from the very beginning, tho they cluster around the times of major releases. I can try to do look this up at some point (like, when my own PC hasn't lost the Internets)... Do you have the ability to make hi-res scans, V?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Mar 09, 2008 8:07 pm Yes our club is in the basement near a computer lab with a scanner. There's something like 10 years worth of Japanese Newtypes in there. I will try the tried and true method of "I don't speak a word of Japanese, so I'll flip through every page until I see a picture of something with Eva on it"

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Mar 10, 2008 1:49 pm Newtypes they have: August 1994 July 1996 September 1996 December 1996 Janaury 1997 (contains a 2 page Eva interview) May 1997 July 1997 August 1997 September 1997 October 1997 March 1998 Febrary 2001 March 2001 April 2001 May 2001 June 2001 September 2001 November 2001 December 2001 January 2002 February 2002 March 2002 April 2002 May 2002 June 2002 July 2002 September 2002 October 2002 November 2002 December 2002 January 2003 March 2003 April 2003 May 2003 June 2003 August 2003 September 2003 November 2003 February 2004 April 2004 June 2004 July 2004 October 2004 January 2005 March 2005 June 2005 July 2005 September 2005 October 2005 November 2005 December 2005 January 2006 February 2006 March 2006 May 2006 Further, They've got the Newtype Evangelion 10th Anniversary collection book, printed in 2005, which is 150 pages long and completely filled with concept art and exclusive interviews. I will be scanning it (unless it is already scanned...)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 10, 2008 2:28 pm Holy llama, that's a lot of stuff! I've also never heard of this Newtype Anniversary thing; I'll need to look into it. Could you maybe lay out a general overview of the contents for me? I'm very curious... In any case, these would expand our resource base exponentially . If we can get good enough scans, that'd remove a lot of pending items from the "Yahoo JP" laundry list. V, have you had any prior experience with scanning? There is a certain "art" to it.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Mar 10, 2008 4:18 pm If you manage to pull this off gloriously, I salute you in name of all eva-fans

[]:             Quote Post by zimlord » Mon Mar 10, 2008 5:24 pm on the topic of Newtype... i just got an issue of something called PiQ magazine today. casually i extracted it from ye olde mailbox and began to flip through it. 'yeah' i thought, 'another anime magazine that fails in it's attempt to be newtype.' it even had some crap about the sarah connor chronicles and sweeney todd--not what i want in my anime mag. then i found a notice stapled to a page. 'what's this?' i thought as I began to read. Turns out Newtype is done and PiQ's taking its place. shit, i mean, seriously, newtype was awesome. looks like it's no more free dvd's, no more kobato, no more AOI house, no more christmas... PiQ might be decent. i'll take a closer look, but for now it seems like a queen's been replaced with a bishop. don't know if this is old news or not, just felt like a little rant was in order... so a few scans would be nice, V. you know, to remind me of the good ol days.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Mar 10, 2008 6:32 pm PiQ Magazine I believe is ADV's bastardized successor to NewTypeUSA, focusing on "more generalized "cool stuff" that "the young people" like, like video games and music" than anime. Basically, like a million others. I will make a page by page description tomorrow. There were no drastically new images; most of the concept art was stuff we've seen re-issued in the Newtype Filmbooks later (Zeruel making fists with his hands, etc.) But it was pretty good; wedged in the back of an old cabinet we haven't opened in months, filled with old Newtypes. One image that stuck out in my mind was a Gainax-made fanart of the three Bridge Bunnies in tropical attire on beach chairs (haha) it was really, in terms of images, nothing that particularly has not appeared elsewhere. The interviews on the other hand, were quite extensive, and featured the entire production team.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Mar 10, 2008 7:37 pm Scans, please?

[]:             Quote Post by RandomStuff » Mon Mar 10, 2008 8:19 pm Damnit! I was gonna subscribe to NewType.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 03, 2008 7:30 pm Still any plans to make scans, V?

Does anyone know what might be issues that contain important Evangelion info? Or even trivial-though-interesting Eva info? I think they have both the English ones as well
